entries.speaker: cael
lines: You're still up.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: This alignment won't set itself. Ley resonance
frequencies are off by point-three. If I don't recalibrate
before the energy cycles—

entries.speaker: lira
lines: You look terrible.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: I'm fine.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: You've said that three times this week. What is it?

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Do you ever think about what happens after?

entries.speaker: lira
lines: After what?

entries.speaker: cael
lines: After all of this. The Pendulum, the ley lines, the
politics. After it's done. What does the world look like?

entries.speaker: lira
lines: Better. The ley lines stabilize. The Forgewright craft
evolves instead of extracting. People build things that work with
the world instead of against it.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: And us?

entries.speaker: lira
lines: We figure it out. Same as everyone else.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Yeah. We figure it out.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Cael. Can't sleep?

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Something like that.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Grab a practice blade. It helps.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: You've been spending too much time in that vault.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Someone has to study it.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Aldis can study it. You should rest.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Aldis doesn't hear it the way I do.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Hear what?

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Nothing. Just... the resonance patterns. They're
louder at night.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Same time tomorrow?

entries.speaker: cael
lines: ...Same time tomorrow.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: Cael. It's late for a social call.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: I had questions. About your research on the Pallor's
previous cycles.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: What kind of questions?

entries.speaker: cael
lines: How long can someone hear the Pallor before it becomes
impossible to refuse?

entries.speaker: maren
lines: That depends on the individual. Weeks. Months.
Some resist for years. Why?

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Academic interest. And — can the Pallor reshape reality,
or only perception?

entries.speaker: maren
lines: Perception. It doesn't change what's real. It changes
what you're able to care about. The world stays the same. You
just stop seeing a reason to fight for it.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: And what if the Pendulum doesn't empower the Pallor,
but actually contains it? What if someone could use the connection
to — redirect it?

entries.speaker: maren
lines: Those are not academic questions, Cael.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Maren. I'm trying to understand what we're dealing
with. That's all.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: The Pallor doesn't make deals. It doesn't negotiate.
It offers what you want most, and the price is always the same —
you stop fighting. Whatever you're thinking, stop thinking it.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: *(internal)* One more. I can see one more before—

entries.speaker: cael
lines: *(internal)* The whisper is louder tonight. Not words.
A feeling. Like the floor is tilting and everyone else is standing
straight but you know it's tilting.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: *(internal)* Enough. It's time.
